old_value: Hydroids, ascidians and sparse algae on silty boulders in the upper circalittoral. Balanus crenatus relatively dense with patches of Morchellium argus and tufts of erect bryozoans and hydroids.
new_value: Porifera indet crusts has been given a nominal abundance value of Present for this record as in Arev it had no abundance value. Small boulders and cobbles in a matrix of pebbles and shell gravel. The boulders and cobbles were covered in Balanus crenatus, there were many patches of an encrusting sponge. Conspicuous was Alcyonium digitatum, Polycarpa pomaria and Morchellium argus. Many species present in low abundance making this a rich biotope. Sagartia elegans was also conspicuous.
N side of Maen Mellt (Morfa Nefyn)

old_value: Upper circalittoral bedrock, boulders and cobbles with a covering of sand in some areas. Hemimycale columella, Nemertesia spp., Aplidium densum, Polymastia mammilaris and a few Ciocalypta penicillus. Algae included Gymnogongrus crenulatus- a SW species.
new_value: Dense bryozoans especially Crisia denticulata, Bicellariella ciliata and Bugula turbinata with barnacles, colonial ascidians and Dendrodoa grossularia and sponges, especially Stelligera rigida and Raspailia ramosa.

old_value: Small cave (4 m long, 1 m high) with abundant Caryophyllia smithii on the roof and walls. Also sheets of thin encrusting sponges, especially Aplysilla sulfurea (both colour morphs), small patches of Thymosia guernei and Galathea strigosa in cracks and crevices in the roof.
new_value: Silty lower infralittoral boulders. Dominant species included Plocamium cartilagineum, Phylophora crispa and Drachiella heterocarpa. Animals included Balanus crenatus and Dendrodoa grossularia covering the silty rock. Others included Dysidea fragilis, Morchellium argus and Hemimycale columella.

old_value: Vertical wall beneath the kelp park, 2 m high and smaller verticals slightly deeper were dominated by bryozoans, particularly Chartella papyracea, Bugula plumosa, Crisia eburnea, sponges especially Dysidea fragilis and colonial ascidians.
new_value: Lower infralittoral bedrock. Dominating the rather silty surface was dense Plocamium cartilaginum. The other conspicuous alga was Corallina officinalis. Characterising animals included Dysidea fragilis, Nemertesia antennina Morchellium argus and little growths of Stelligera rigida.

old_value: Small cave (4 m long, 1 m high) with abundant Caryophyllia smithii on the roof and walls. Also sheets of thin encrusting sponges, especially Aplysilla sulfurea (both colour morphs), small patches of Thymosia guernei and Galathea strigosa in cracks and crevices in the roof.
new_value: Lower circalittoral bedrock and boulders. Dominant species were clumps of Nemertesia antennina and Gymnangium montagui and huge clumps of Cliona celata. Also conspicuous dotted around the seabed were colonies of Axinella polypoides. The bedrock was silty and Balanus crenatus covered with much Cellaria sinuosa.

old_value: Exposed circalittoral bedrock with a scour fauna of Sertularia argentea, Balanus crenatus and lots of Alcyonidium diaphanum colonies. Also many small patches of Esperiopsis fucorum. One limestone boulder with Hiatella arctica and Ciona intestinalis in the holes.
new_value: Exposed upper circalittoral stepped bedrock with abundant Diphasia rosacea and Nemertesia antennina. Also covering the rocks were a wide variety of bryozoans, noticeably Scrupocellaria spp. and Crisia spp. and sponges (Polymastia spp., Stelligera rigida and Raspalia ramosa).

old_value: Dense bryozoans especially Crisia denticulata, Bicellariella ciliata and Bugula turbinata with barnacles, colonial ascidians and Dendrodoa grossularia and sponges, especially Stelligera rigida and Raspailia ramosa.
new_value: An exposed vertical limestone wall dominated by a dense bryozoan turf including Bugula turbinata, Scrupocellaria spp., Bicellariella ciliata and Cellaria sinuosa. Dense patches of Parazoanthus axinellae were also present along with a variety of sponges.

old_value: A mixed substratum characterised by boulders of various sizes with a rich fauna and some red algae. The fauna was characterised by sponges, for example Polymastia boletiformis and also Tethyspira spinosa. Ascidians included the small, as yet unnamed `pin head squirt` and the also unnamed `Strawberry squirt`. Smaller rocks had some scour with indicators being Flustra foliacea and Vesicularia spp. Of note was Asterina phylactica and the ascidian Polysyncraton lacazei.

Dominant species were Desmarestia viridis, Laminaria sporelings, Dictyota dichotoma with various red algae including Phyllophora crispa and Delesseria sanguinea. Conspicuous animals were Sertularia cupressina and Diphasia rosacea.

new_value: Bedrock gullies and ridges with kelp park with an understory of sparse sponge- Polymastia boletiformis and Hemimycale columella, coralline crusts and sparse red algae. Also patches of Balanus crenatus and Crisiids.

Boulders and cobbles in the infralittoral were surrounded by and scoured by coarse sand. The algae were dominated by ephemeral or sand scour resistant species such as Laminaria saccharina, Halidrys siliquosa, Phyllophora crispa and Dilsea carnosa. Sabellaria spinulosa frequently found beneath smothering sand.
new_value: Overhanging bedrock. Large Pachymatisma johstonia and areas of a yellow/brown encrusting sponge. Also sheets of white Diplosoma spongiforme were conspicuous. Elsewhere the rock was covered in bryozoan turf in which Crisia denticulata, Bugula tubinata and Bugula flabellata being the main species.

old_value: Tentorium semisuberites has been given a nominal abundance value of Present for this record as in Arev it had no abundance value. The upper part of the lower infralittoral, below the kelp forest proper. This zone was bedrock with some clean shell gravel. Dominant species were Desmarestia viridis, Laminaria sporelings, Dictyota dichootma with various red algae including Phyllophora crispa and Delesseria sanguinea. Conspicuous animals were Sertularia cupressina and Diphasia rosacea.
